articles.title: Memphis House Primary Exemplifies Democrats’ Age Fights
articles.summary: A primary in Tennessee between Representative Steve Cohen, a white incumbent, and Justin Pearson, a Black state lawmaker, exemplifies a national push for a passing of the torch.

articles.title: After Israel's invasion, many in southern Lebanon worry they'll never go home
articles.summary: Israel says it's creating a "security zone" inside Lebanon, where homes will be demolished, and residents won't be allowed to return home until people in northern Israel feel safe.

articles.title: South Dakotans React to Daily Mail Article on Bryon Noem, Kristi Noem’s Husband
articles.summary: In the tiny town of Castlewood, S.D., where everyone knows the Noems, the prevailing sense was that people can’t help but feel bad for Bryon Noem after a tabloid photo leak.

articles.title: How to Watch NASA’s Artemis II Moon Launch Online
articles.summary: NASA will broadcast the launch of the Artemis II mission as well as holding live conversations with the crew during their time in space.

articles.title: Placing U.S. Troops in Middle East Hotels May Violate Laws of War
articles.summary: U.S. commanders have kept many troops away from bases in the region to protect them from Iran’s ballistic missile attacks.

articles.title: Trump Says Europe Is On Its Own in Securing the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Threatening to pull out of NATO, President Trump portrayed the alliance as a “paper tiger” and said Europe was on its own in trying to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Israel’s Message to a Broad Swath of Lebanon: Shiites Must Go
articles.summary: Israel has issued sweeping evacuation warnings, and pressed some Christian and Druse leaders to expel Shiite Muslims from southern towns, the leaders said.

articles.title: Greetings from London, where a big COVID memorial holds vigil over the halls of power
articles.summary: Britain's 1,640-foot-long National Covid Memorial Wall stretches along the River Thames, with over a quarter million hearts representing the lives lost during the pandemic — across from Parliament.

articles.title: The oil industry is betting big on plastics. Here's what that means for the future
articles.summary: Journalist Beth Gardiner says the fossil fuel industry is increasingly reliant upon plastic products. Her book is Plastic Inc.: The Secret History and Shocking Future of Big Oil's Biggest Bet.

articles.title: Trump to address nation on Iran war. And, SCOTUS considers birthright citizenship
articles.summary: Trump is set to address the nation after saying the U.S. will withdraw from Iran in 2-3 weeks. And, the president will be at the Supreme Court today as arguments on birthright citizenship begin.

articles.title: Trump will address the nation after saying the U.S. may leave the Iran war within weeks
articles.summary: President Trump is set to address the nation on the Iran war Wednesday night and provide an "important update," White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said.

articles.title: From scrappy startup to tech giant, Apple celebrates its 50th year
articles.summary: Countercultural mythmaking and global corporate dominance have helped the tech corporation sail through criticism.

articles.title: 4 takeaways from the U.S. men's national soccer team's World Cup tune-up games
articles.summary: The two losses — 5-2 to Belgium and 2-0 to Portugal — were a wakeup call for the USMNT and the team's ambitions for this summer's World Cup. But the Americans said they welcomed the lessons learned.

articles.title: Homesick in a foreign country, a teenager meets a lifelong friend
articles.summary: Holly Deiaco-Smith was feeling homesick while studying abroad in France when she was 19 years old. An encounter at the post office changed everything and led to a decades-long friendship.

articles.title: After years in Russian captivity, former mayor of Ukrainian city returns home
articles.summary: After spending most of the full-scale war in Russian captivity, the former mayor of a frontline Ukrainian city recalls his ordeal as he returns to a hometown deeply transformed by modern warfare.

articles.title: Food assistance slashed for hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees trapped in Bangladesh camps
articles.summary: Hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees struggling to survive in Bangladesh's overcrowded camps will see their food assistance slashed starting on Wednesday, raising alarm throughout the increasing

articles.title: Trump Signs Order Seeking Federal Control of Mail Voting as He Promotes False Claims
articles.summary: Election experts and Democratic officials called the order legally invalid, and Arizona and Oregon pledged to immediately challenge it in court.

articles.title: Kherson, Ukraine: A hometown changed (Pt. 1)
articles.summary: Four years of Russia's all-out war on Ukraine have transformed not only Ukrainian cities but also how modern warfare is waged, in the first of this two-part story from Kherson.

articles.title: Why the Artemis II Astronauts Will Be Wearing Orange
articles.summary: It’s not just any orange. It’s International Orange.

articles.title: Iranians debate whether the war is worth it
articles.summary: As the war in Iran enters its second month, and President Trump signals an end to the war, many Iranians are urging the U.S and Israel to keep striking their country.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
